title: linaria oblongifolia subsp. aragonensis
intro: Linaria oblongifolia subsp. aragonensis, also known as Aragon toadflax, is a subspecies of the Linaria oblongifolia plant, a flowering plant that belongs to the figwort family. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues and as an expectorant to help relieve congestion. The health benefits of Linaria oblongifolia subsp. aragonensis include reducing inflammation, soothing coughs, and providing relief from symptoms associated with the common cold. In herbal preparations, Linaria oblongifolia subsp. aragonensis can be used in teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
